title: The Last Warning

evidence.note: Published 1928; the 95-year US copyright term has expired for works published through 1930, so the film print is public domain in the US by term expiry.

diy_links.label: Stanford Copyright Renewal DB — search “The Last Warning” (renewals ~1955, 1956, 1957)

US Copyright Office public records (1978+ renewals/assignments)

editorial: The Last Warning is a 1928 American mystery-thriller directed by Paul Leni for Universal Pictures, starring Laura La Plante in a backstage whodunit staged on the recycled sets of Universal's Phantom of the Opera. Released as a part-talkie, it survives today only in its cut-down silent version and was Leni's final film before his death in 1929. As a 1928 US publication, the film print is public domain by expiry of the 95-year US copyright term; other layers remain undetermined.
